title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Inflation Concerns Dampen Sentiment
subtitle: April 09, 2026 - Not financial advice

items.headline: US Markets Fluctuate Amid Renewed Geopolitical Concerns, Oil Prices Climb Towards $100
items.summary: US stock markets saw mixed movements on Thursday, initially dipping before a partial recovery, as investor sentiment remained cautious. Renewed tensions between the US and Iran, coupled with rising crude oil prices, overshadowed earlier optimism surrounding a potential ceasefire. Brent crude climbed towards $98-$100 per barrel, fueling inflation worries and impacting the outlook for interest rate policies.

items.statContext: Initially slipped 0.1%, later reversed to a gain of 0.27% to 0.3%.
Fell 40 points or 0.1% early, then rose 61-77 points or 0.14%-0.2%.
Declined 0.2% in early trade, later tacked on 0.3% or 67 points.

items.headline: Constellation Brands
items.summary: The beverage alcohol company surged after reporting stronger-than-expected quarterly results.

items.headline: CoreWeave
items.summary: Rallied after announcing an expanded, multi-billion dollar deal with Meta Platforms to provide AI cloud capacity.

items.headline: Lumentum Holdings
items.summary: Shares climbed after JPMorgan reiterated an overweight rating on the stock with a price target of $950.

items.headline: Simply Good Foods
items.summary: Plunged following a sharper-than-expected drop in revenue, with the CEO expressing dissatisfaction.

items.headline: Figma
items.summary: Experienced a significant meltdown as AI tools automate design, eroding investor confidence in SaaS companies despite strong revenue growth.

items.headline: Duolingo
items.summary: Suffered a brutal decline amidst the 'SaaS-pocalypse' triggered by AI competition, despite reporting strong user growth and revenue.

items.headline: Inflation Remains Elevated, Jobless Claims Rise
items.summary: The February Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price report showed both headline and core inflation up 0.4% month-over-month, with annual core inflation at 3%. This suggests inflation is not slowing, a concern for the Federal Reserve. Separately, weekly jobless claims rose more than anticipated, potentially signaling a softening labor market.

items.headline: Fed Officials Consider Rate Hike Amid Persistent Inflation Risks
items.summary: Minutes from the latest FOMC meeting revealed a hawkish bias, with many participants expressing concerns over higher energy prices and their implications for inflation. Some officials are even considering the possibility of a rate hike, a significant shift from earlier expectations of rate cuts in 2026, as inflation remains stubbornly above the Fed's 2% target.

items.headline: Energy Leads, Tech and Consumer Discretionary Lag Amid Market Rotation
items.summary: Energy ($XLE) emerged as a leading sector, driven by surging oil prices and geopolitical tensions. Other leading or improving sectors include Materials ($XLB), Industrials ($XLI), Consumer Staples ($XLP), Utilities ($XLU), Real Estate ($XLRE), Communication Services ($XLC), and Financials ($XLF). Conversely, Technology ($XLK) and Consumer Discretionary ($XLY) are lagging, partly due to investor concerns over AI competition impacting software companies.

items.headline: Fourth Quarter GDP Revised Lower to 0.5%
items.summary: The U.S. economy grew at an annualized rate of 0.5% in the fourth quarter, a downward revision from earlier estimates and below economists' expectations. This lower figure reflects a weaker economic expansion, primarily driven by a downward revision in investment.
